---
title: "Papers"
description: >-
  Papers in the A=1 Discrete Causal Lattice series, with Zenodo DOIs,
  arXiv links, and repositories — discrete spacetime, emergent Lorentz
  invariance, and the Standard Model gauge group from geometry.
page-layout: full
---

Every paper in the A=1 Discrete Causal Lattice series. Each entry
points to the canonical Zenodo deposit (where applicable), the
working repository, and the current version.

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
**New to the series?** The [**Claim map**](claim-map.qmd) is a
one-page, reviewer-facing summary of what Papers I and II *prove* versus
what they *conjecture* — every claim labelled and tied to its audit tag.
:::

The series is collected in the author's
[A=1 Discrete Causal Lattice](https://zenodo.org/communities/a1-discrete-causal-lattice)
community on Zenodo. Individual papers are also featured in
independently curated communities:
[Geometric Foundations of Quantum Theory and Gravity](https://zenodo.org/communities/fbt-framework)
(both papers) and
[Quantum Gravity Research (Open Source)](https://zenodo.org/communities/osqgr)
(Paper I).

## Published

### Paper I — *Geometry First*

The substrate-priority paper. Establishes the discrete bipartite
octahedral lattice as the ontological substrate and recovers Lorentz
invariance as an O$_h$-averaged consequence of the tick rule.

- **Version:** v1.01
- **DOI:** [10.5281/zenodo.20291342](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20291342)
- **Prior-version DOI:** [10.5281/zenodo.20078529](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20078529) (v1.0)
- **Repository:** [`dcl`](https://github.com/JackDMenendez/dcl)
- **Landing page:** [Paper I — Geometry First](paper-01-geometry-first.qmd)

### Paper II — *Geometry Forces Physics*

Characterises the Standard Model gauge group $SU(3) \times SU(2) \times U(1)$
as the *factor-product projection* of the lattice's 71-dimensional
automorphism algebra — containment holds, exact equality does not.
Predicts the coupling ratio $g_3^2/g_2^2 = 3/2$ at the lattice scale and
leaves the universal gauge-coupling prefactor $c$ open; its closure is
the centerpiece of the planned discrete-probability paper.

- **Version:** v1.01
- **DOI:** [10.5281/zenodo.20292158](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20292158)
- **Prior-version DOI:** [10.5281/zenodo.20240736](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20240736) (v1.0)
- **Repository:** [`dcl-sm-derivation`](https://github.com/JackDMenendez/dcl-paper-02-sm-derivation)
- **Landing page:** [Paper II — Geometry Forces Physics](paper-02-geometry-forces-physics.qmd)

### `dcl-core` — Python engine

The simulation engine that underlies the experiment scripts in the
paper repositories. Ships **two engines under one import**:
`dcl_core.core` is a verbatim port of Paper I's continuous-amplitude
implementation; `dcl_core.core3d` is a new integer-token
reimplementation that enforces A=1 by integer arithmetic — Paper III
will compare them side-by-side.

- **Version:** v0.1.0
- **DOI:** [10.5281/zenodo.20350952](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20350952)
- **Repository:** [`dcl-core`](https://github.com/JackDMenendez/dcl-core)
- **Pinning syntax for downstream papers:**
  `dcl_core @ git+https://github.com/JackDMenendez/dcl-core@v0.1.0`

## In progress

### Paper III — *Tidal Ionization and the Quantum Roche Limit*

A third ionization channel (gradient detuning) alongside the
standard thermal (Saha) and pressure (Pauli) channels. Predicts an
atomic ISCO with two observational signatures (21 cm HI inner-edge
morphology near compact objects, Fe K$\alpha$ line-profile inner
edge in accretion disks).

- **Version:** v0.1 (draft)
- **Repository:** [`dcl-paper-03-tidal-ionization`](https://github.com/JackDMenendez/dcl-paper-03-tidal-ionization)

### `dcl-generator-zoo` — Catalogue of the 71-dim per-site automorphism algebra

A standalone catalog paper / artifact for the lattice's automorphism
algebra; consumed by Paper II's gauge derivation and the planned
proton-internals paper.

- **Version:** v0.1 (draft)
- **Repository:** [`dcl-generator-zoo`](https://github.com/JackDMenendez/dcl-generator-zoo)

## Planned

The following subprojects are in the framework's forward-looking
design but not yet drafted:

- **Discrete-probability paper** — empirical companion to
  `dcl_core.core3d` v1.0; the centerpiece showcase closes Paper II's
  open gauge-coupling prefactor.
- **`dcl-formalism` package + paper** — a sympy-extending formalism
  package (axioms / theorems / continuum-limit primitives) and
  companion paper. Designed to support the Hilbert-Sixth capstone.
- **Hilbert-Sixth axiomatization** — *Geometry Axiomatizes Physics*.
  The methodological capstone tying Papers I → II → III →
  discrete-probability → `dcl-formalism` into a single axiomatic
  treatment.
- **Proton internals** — a future paper that consumes Paper II's
  SU(3) machinery and the generator-zoo catalog.
